title: Song Of The Dispossessed. "To Jesus."
content:
"Be with us by day, by night,
        O lover, O friend;
     Hold before us thy light
        Unto the end!

     "See, all these children of ours
        Starved and ill-clad.
     Speak to thy heart's lily-flowers,
        And make them glad!

     "Our wives and daughters are here,
        Knowing wrong and shame's touch
     Bid them be of good cheer
        Who have loved much.

     "And we, we are robbed and oppressed,
        Even as thine were.
     Tell us of comfort and rest,
        Banish despair!

     "Be with us by day, by night,
        O lover, O friend;
     Hold before us thy light
        Unto the end!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Francis William Lauderdale Adams, titled "Song Of The Dispossessed. "To Jesus."", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
